line_preview: ##@@ INSTRUCTIONS @@##
# Please do not remove any comments from this file, but feel free to add as many
# comments to R code as you like!
# Replace all ---USER INPUT--- sections with relevant input (including the -
# symbols).
# When you are done, save this file as RPCB_Final_Analysis_Study_XX.R, where XX
# is the study number.
# e.g., The first study (https://osf.io/yyqas/),
# "Study 1: Replication of Poliseno et al., 2010 (Nature)", should be
# called "RPCB_Final_Analysis_Study_1.R"
##@@ GENERAL INFORMATION @@##
#@ Original Study Title: ---ENTER ORIGINAL STUDY TITLE HERE---
#@ OSF link project: ---ENTER OSF LINK TO PROJECT---
#@ eLife link to Registered Report: ---ENTER DOI TO REGISTERED REPORT---
#@ eLife link to Replication Study: ---ENTER DOI TO REPLICATION STUDY---
#@ Experiment and Effect Numbers: ---ENTER NUMBER OF EXPERIMENTS (EFFECTS)---
#                           e.g., Experiment 1 (3 effects)
#                                 Experiment 2 (1 effect)
##@@ REQUIRED PACKAGES @@##
# R Version X.X.X
library(httr) # version
library(rjson) # version
#source("~/Credentials.R") #for private use during initial R script generation
#In the function download.OSF.file, 'RPCB_private_access' is an object that contains the private viewing link or a private access token
#which is necessary to view and create scripts while this project is private. Upon publication, when the project is made public,
#a private access token or private link will no longer be necessary.
#Downloads R script "download.OSF.file.R"
GET("https://osf.io/hkpjb/?action=download", write_disk("download.OSF.file.R", overwrite = TRUE))
source("download.OSF.file.R") #calls the download.OSF.file function
##@@ EXPERIMENT 1 (X EFFECTS) @@##
##@@ DATA LOADING @@##
#@ NOTE: Data must be loaded from OSF directly and NOT rely on any local files.
#@ Original study data
---ENTER R CODE TO LOAD ORIGINAL DATA FROM OSF---
#@ Replication study data
---ENTER R CODE TO LOAD REPLICATION DATA FROM OSF---
##@@ EFFECT 1 @@##
##@@ REPRESENTATIVE ORIGINAL DATA @@##
#@ Was original data representative? ---ENTER YES/NO FOR ORIGINAL DATA---
#@ If YES (Original data is representative):
#@ NOTE: Delete this section if original data is not representative
##@@ POINT DIFFERENCE @@##
#@ Original point difference
---ENTER R CODE THAT EVALUATES TO THE POINT DIFFERENCE FOR ORIGINAL DATA---
#@ Replication raw difference: ---ENTER IF RAW DIFFERENCE IS MEAN OR MEDIAN---
---ENTER R CODE THAT EVALUATES TO THE RAW DIFFERENCE FOR REPLICATION DATA---
##@@ SAMPLE SIZE @@##
#@ Replication sample size
#@ NOTE: If one sample or paired, put total sample size for GROUP 1 and NA for GROUP 2
---ENTER R CODE THAT EVALUATES TO THE GROUP 1 SAMPLE SIZE FOR REPLICATION DATA---
---ENTER R CODE THAT EVALUATES TO THE GROUP 2 SAMPLE SIZE FOR REPLICATION DATA---
##@@ 95% CONFIDENCE INTERVAL @@##

#@ NOTE: If one sample or paired, put SD for GROUP 1 and NA for GROUP 2
